plugins.name: ai-toolkit-dev
plugins.description: Toolkit for authoring Claude Code plugins, marketplaces, and skills (work in progress)

plugins.name: project-setup
plugins.description: Architectural decision-maker for repos — new and existing. Triggers on any layout / structural / convention decision: where a file or folder belongs, monorepo vs polyrepo, adding or splitting services, per-app stack choice, picking a database, docker / compose layout, env-config split, secrets, design tokens, modularity, AI / LLM integration, security hardening, mobile / desktop / PWA targets, ML cloud orchestration. Bootstrap AND restructure. One slash command (/ps-setup) with init | audit | suggest modes. Composes with the sibling agent-ks plugin.

plugins.name: agent-ks
plugins.description: Operating manual for the agent-knowledge-system framework — writing markdown, configuring site.yaml/navbar.yaml/footer.yaml, working with the issue tracker, blog posts, themes, building HTML artifacts, and anything under the project's data/ folder. Ships 3 skills: agent-ks-docs (docs/blog/config/writing/themes — triages tasks to domain-specific reference files: writing, docs-layout, blog-layout, settings-layout, images), agent-ks-issues (the complete, self-contained issue-tracker skill — anatomy [brainstorm/notes/subtasks/agent-log/agent-memory/comments/glossary], creation threshold rules + the issue dump, lifecycle + AI rules, activity-folder agent-logs, tracker-flavoured writing reference; also fires on the execution verbs audit/refactor/loop/discuss against a tracked issue), and agent-ks-artifacts (building self-contained HTML artifacts — reports, dashboards, data visualizations, design systems / brand guidelines — as NN_ .html content files served at /artifacts with a .meta.json sidecar: treatment calibration, honoring the host theme contract, dual-theme + self-containment discipline, the dataviz procedure + bundled palette validator, and design-system authoring flows; NOT claude.ai Artifacts). Plus 28 CLI commands behind one collision-safe `agent-ks` dispatcher — the single entrypoint on PATH — each invoked as `agent-ks <group> <verb>` with a uniform contract (--help/-h, --json, exit codes): issue tracker (issue list/show/subtasks/agent-logs/set-state/add-comment/add-agent-log/review-queue), validators (check blog/config/section/issues/skill-links), docs+blog content (doc list/show/search, blog list/show/search), git-derived metadata (git updated/changed/log/commit, guarded), cross-content search (find), move for link-aware moves, img to optimize images, help for discovery, and resolve-context. (The flat `docs-*` names are retired to internal manifest ids.) Plus 3 slash commands (/agent-ks-init to bootstrap a new docs project, /agent-ks-add-section to scaffold a new top-level section, /agent-ks-quick-idea-note to capture an ad-hoc idea into the issue dump). Cross-platform CLI: bash + .cmd shims for Linux/macOS/WSL and native Windows; polyglot-ready dispatcher.

plugins.name: dstack
plugins.description: Upstream dstack skill (vendor-tracked from dstackai/dstack). dstack is an open-source control plane for GPU provisioning and orchestration across GPU clouds, Kubernetes, and on-prem clusters — running dev environments, tasks, and services, managing fleets/volumes/gateways, and applying `*.dstack.yml` configurations via the `dstack` CLI.

plugins.name: uvenv
plugins.description: Operating manual for uvenv — Sid's bash/zsh wrapper around mise + uv that adds conda-style named global Python venvs you can activate from any directory. Covers the flags-first grammar (uvenv <subcommand> [uvenv-flags] <action> [verbatim args passed to uv/mise]), the four env kinds (base mise python, uv-managed tools, named global venvs, local in-tree venvs), confirm-on-create UX, conda→uvenv command translation, doctor-first diagnostics, and the safety rails. Triggers on any mention of `uvenv`, named global Python venvs, conda replacement, or migrating off conda/miniconda/anaconda/mamba while keeping conda-like ergonomics.
